_guide._about: config.json drives everything in the Competitive Intelligence Dashboard — competitor profiles, channel activity ratings, event attendance, and RSS data sources for automated daily briefs.
_howToAddACompetitor: Copy an existing competitor block from the 'competitors' array.
Give it a unique 'id' (lowercase, no spaces — used as a key throughout).
Fill in as many data sources as you can (see _dataSources below).
Set channelActivity levels honestly: h=high, m=medium, l=low, n=none.
Add event attendance entries for any events they've been seen at.

googleAlertRssUrl.what: The RSS feed URL from a Google Alert you set up for this company.
googleAlertRssUrl.why: This is the primary automated data source — the daily brief script fetches it every day and feeds the headlines to the AI for analysis.
howToGet: Search: "Company Name" (use quotes for exact match)
Optional: refine to 'Only the best results', 'Any source', 'Any language'
Click 'Show options', select 'RSS feed' as delivery method
Copy the RSS URL and paste it here
googleAlertRssUrl.linkedinTip: Add a second alert: site:linkedin.com "Company Name" — this picks up their LinkedIn posts via Google index.
googleAlertRssUrl.nullMeans: No alert set up yet. Script will show 'no RSS' for this competitor.

youtubeRssUrl.what: YouTube channel RSS feed URL — auto-fetched for new video titles.
howToGetChannelId: Go to their YouTube channel page
Ctrl+F for 'channel_id' or 'externalId' — copy the UC... value
Or use a tool like commentpicker.com/youtube-channel-id.php
youtubeRssUrl.nullMeans: No YouTube channel found or not set up yet.

blogRssUrl.what: RSS feed URL for their blog/news section.
howToGet: Try appending /feed or /rss to their blog URL
Check page source for <link rel='alternate' type='application/rss+xml'>
Most enterprise BSS/OSS companies have dropped public RSS — Google Alert is the fallback

_dataSources.linkedinUrl: Their LinkedIn company page — used for channel activity tracking and the channel matrix link.
_dataSources.newsroomUrl: Their press releases or news page — used as the link-out for press/blog channel activity.

_channelActivityLevels.h: High — frequent, consistent activity (e.g. 3+/wk for LinkedIn, 4+/mo for blog)
_channelActivityLevels.m: Medium — regular but not dominant (e.g. 1-2×/wk LinkedIn, 2×/mo blog)
_channelActivityLevels.l: Low — occasional or sparse (e.g. 1×/mo or quarterly)
_channelActivityLevels.n: None — no meaningful presence on this channel

_eventAttendance.what: Record when a competitor appears at a tracked industry event.
_eventAttendance.keyFormat: Use the event 'id' from the 'events' array (e.g. 'MWC26', 'DTW25').

fields.note: What they did there — keynote, booth, award, session, etc. Keep to 1 sentence.
fields.url: Link to a press release, blog post, or Google search confirming their presence.

_eventAttendance.tip: If you spot a competitor at an event (LinkedIn post, press release, conference agenda), add it here. It feeds the Event Presence matrix on the dashboard.

_tierDefinitions.enterprise: Global BSS/OSS vendors with $100M+ revenue — Netcracker, Amdocs, Comarch. Rarely a direct competitor but set the narrative.
_tierDefinitions.mid-market: Regional/growing vendors with $20M–$100M revenue — CSG, Covalense, Cerillion, Optiva, Wavelo. Most frequent real competition.
_tierDefinitions.peer: Small/niche vendors of similar scale to CloudSmartz — Amartus, Orchest, Beesion, Everstage. Direct pipeline overlap.

_eventsNote: Add events here to track competitor attendance. Each event gets a column in the Event Presence matrix. Use a short unique 'id' — this is the key used in each competitor's 'eventAttendance' map. 'date' must be YYYY-MM for correct timeline sorting.
_competitorsNote: Each competitor object drives one column in every dashboard table. Required: id, name, tier. Everything else improves signal quality. Add 'googleAlertRssUrl' first — it feeds the automated daily brief. See _guide._dataSources for how to get each URL type.

competitors.name: Netcracker Technology
competitors.notes: Digital BSS/OSS, Agentic AI lead narrative. NEC subsidiary. Most active content machine.

MWC26.note: Agentic AI sessions and NXO platform demos on the show floor

FN26.note: Won 2026 FutureNet Award for Autonomous Network Scaling with Swisscom

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 — keynote sponsor and Catalyst contributor

GAR25.note: Noted in Gartner Telecom 2025 reports and Magic Quadrant

SCTE25.note: SCTE TechExpo 2025 presenter on BSS transformation

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Most active content machine in the space. Agentic AI narrative dominant. CloudSmartz wins on mid-market agility, CPQ specificity, and positioning as NEC-independent.

competitors.name: Amdocs
competitors.notes: Enterprise BSS/OSS, GenAI (amAIz). $4.6B revenue. AWS partnership MWC 2026.

MWC26.note: Multi-year AWS partnership announced; aOS platform keynote sessions

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 participant and sponsor

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Different category — targets AT&T-scale operators. Rarely competes directly. CloudSmartz wins: mid-market speed, CPQ specificity, cost. Watch for any down-market moves.

competitors.name: Comarch
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Comarch telecom' and paste the RSS URL here. See _guide._dataSources.googleAlertRssUrl for steps.
competitors.notes: Full BSS/OSS suite. Large Polish IT company, WSE-listed. In 4 Gartner 2025 telecom reports. 30+ years. AI-driven BSS narrative (Telco Republic 2025).

MWC26.note: Exhibit presence and 5G BSS/OSS sessions at MWC Barcelona 2026

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 — TM Forum Platinum Partner (20 API certs)

ATA26.note: Asian Telecom Awards 2026 — regional BSS presence

GAR25.note: Featured in 4 separate Gartner telecom market reports (2025)

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Strong analyst presence and full BSS/OSS suite. TM Forum Platinum Partner. CloudSmartz wins on mid-market agility, faster time-to-value, and CPQ/NaaS specificity.

competitors.name: CSG International
competitors.notes: BSS/billing. Acquired by NEC Oct 2025 ($2.89B). NEC integration underway.

MWC26.note: CSG 'Radical Simplicity' AI CX theme — booth sessions at MWC 2026

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 participant (pre-NEC acquisition finalization)

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Post-NEC acquisition creates customer anxiety — displacement opportunity now. CloudSmartz wins as the independent, agile alternative with no integration risk.

competitors.name: Covalense Digital
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Covalense Digital' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors._youtubeTodo: YouTube channel ID unverified. Check youtube.com/@CovalenseDigital — if confirmed, add youtubeRssUrl.
competitors.notes: 5G Digital BSS (Csmart). Oracle partner. MVNO/MVNE/IoT focus. 'Digital BSS Trailblazer' Fast Mode 2025. ~$16M revenue, 5 continents.

NASSCOM26.note: NASSCOM / regional India conference 2026 — Oracle partner session

MVNO25.note: MVNO World Congress 2025 — Csmart MVNE/MVNO platform showcase

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: 5G Digital BSS and Oracle partnership create direct SME overlap. CloudSmartz wins on CPQ depth, NaaS specificity, and broader platform scope.

competitors.name: Cerillion
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Cerillion BSS' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors._youtubeTodo: YouTube channel found but confirmed empty/inactive — skip unless activity observed.
competitors.notes: BSS/OSS suite. LSE-listed. ~£60M revenue FY2025. TM Forum ODA-ready. Largest deal ever: Omantel 2026. Fastest-growing in mid-market.

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 — ODA-ready certification showcase

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Fastest-growing mid-market threat. Record £42.5M Omantel deal and A2A AI in v26.1 signal rapid upmarket momentum. CloudSmartz wins on CPQ/NaaS specificity and implementation speed.

competitors.name: Optiva
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Optiva telecom BSS' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors.notes: Cloud-native BSS + charging. TSX-listed. Merged with Qvantel Sept 2025. Agentic AI (Google Gemini). 70 CSPs, 40 countries, 1000+ staff. Direct AI narrative competitor.

MWC26.note: Post-Qvantel merger presence at MWC 2026; Gemini AI BSS demos

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 participant — pre-merger as Optiva

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Post-Qvantel merger spans 70+ CSPs with Gemini AI integration — direct AI narrative threat. CloudSmartz wins on BSS/CPQ depth and implementation agility.

competitors.name: Wavelo
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Wavelo telecom' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors._youtubeTodo: YouTube search 'Wavelo solutions' has name collisions — verify channel ID before adding youtubeRssUrl.
competitors.notes: Cloud-native BSS/OSS. Tucows subsidiary (NASDAQ: TCX). Event-driven architecture. 'Free Your Data' AI initiative Sept 2025. Customers: Boost Mobile, Ting.

MWC26.note: 'Free Your Data' AI initiative launch and Boost Mobile case study at MWC 2026

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: 'Free Your Data' AI initiative targets same data-liberation angle. Strong blog output. CloudSmartz wins on BSS/CPQ depth and telecom domain expertise vs Wavelo's narrower scope.

competitors.name: Amartus
competitors._youtubeTodo: Handle @amartus_sds confirmed but channel_id UCxxx not found — check their YouTube about page to get the channel ID.
competitors.notes: NaaS automation, nBrace platform. TM Forum Outstanding Catalyst 2025.

MEF25.note: MEF GNE 2025 — Outstanding Catalyst Award (SATCOM/EDGE Phase III)

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 — nBrace platform showcase and Catalyst participation

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Peer-level NaaS automation specialist. Winning TM Forum awards = strong community channel. CloudSmartz wins on broader BSS/CPQ scope and Americas footprint.

competitors.name: Orchest Technologies
competitors.notes: CPQ + Network Orchestration PaaS launched Nov 2025. LATAM/Caribbean focus. Direct CPQ overlap.

DTW25.note: TM Forum DTW 2025 — CPQ PaaS platform presence

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Smallest direct CPQ overlap — LATAM/Caribbean focus, newly launched PaaS. Watch for customer announcements in shared geographies.

competitors.name: Beesion
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Beesion telecom' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors.notes: Low-code BSS/OSS (BeeFiber). B2B BSS Full Stack. LATAM/Americas focus. 20 countries. Fort Lauderdale FL.
competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Low-code BSS in shared Americas territory. Minimal public activity. CloudSmartz wins on enterprise integration depth and CPQ specificity.

competitors.name: Everstage
competitors._googleAlertTodo: Set up a Google Alert for 'Everstage SPM' or 'Everstage telecom' and paste the RSS URL here.
competitors.notes: Sales Performance Management (SPM) / Incentive Compensation for Telco. Integrates with BSS/CRM/ERP. Forrester Strong Performer Q1 2025. Different category — tracks sales channel overlap.

FOR25.note: Forrester Wave™ Sales Performance Management Q1 2025 — Strong Performer

competitors.vsCloudSmartz: Adjacent SPM/ICM — not a direct BSS competitor. Relevant if CloudSmartz expands channel incentive automation for telco sales teams. Different buyer persona.
